stories.title: RECALL — Vevor Baby Swings (Suffocation Hazard, Safe Sleep Violation)
stories.angle: CPSC recall of Vevor baby swings sold January–August 2025 on Amazon and Vevor.com ($65–$80); three models (BB501K, BB702A, BB005K); incline angle exceeds 10 degrees, violating mandatory infant sleep products standard and the Safe Sleep for Babies Act inclined sleepers ban; poses deadly suffocation risk; remedy: full refund — destroy swing cover, write "Recalled" on base, send photo to recalling@vevor.com; contact Sanven Technology 855-599-6320, Mon–Fri 9 AM–5 PM PT

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL ALERT: Vevor baby swings sold Jan–Aug 2025 on Amazon + Vevor.com recalled for suffocation risk.

Stop use now. Models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K. Full refund: recalling@vevor.com or 855-599-6320.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Why inclined baby sleepers are dangerous: the Safe Sleep for Babies Act bans them because inclined surfaces cause infants' heads to tip forward, blocking the airway.

Check your swing. Vevor models are recalled.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Vevor Baby Swing Recall: Three Models Recalled for Suffocation Hazard — Stop Use Now

stories.title: COS Parks $3.1 Million Budget Cut — What It Means for Families This Summer
stories.angle: Colorado Springs parks face $3.1 million budget reduction for 2026; damaged playground equipment will be removed instead of replaced (maintenance budget cut to ~$100,000, 50% reduction); 25% of neighborhood park permanent restrooms closed; 50% reduction in playground surfacing materials; BRIGHT SPOT: Flanagan Park (central COS) receiving $750,000 federal community development grant for new playground, ADA-accessible path with lighting and benches, resurfaced basketball court

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
COS parks face a $3.1 million budget cut this summer.

What that means: damaged playground equipment removed not replaced, 25% of park restrooms closed, playground surfacing reduced 50%.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: COS Parks Face $3.1 Million in Budget Cuts — What Families Need to Know This Summer

stories.title: PPYMCA Summer Day Camp Week 2 — "Low-Key Famous Artist Week" June 8–12
stories.angle: Pikes Peak YMCA Summer Day Camp Week 2 runs June 8–12 for ages 5–12; theme is "Low-Key Famous Artist Week" — a week of art, creativity, and summer fun; registration available at ppymca.org; full-day and partial-day options available; financial assistance available through the YMCA scholarship program; Week 1 (June 1–5, "Vintage Colorado: No WiFi Edition") just wrapped

x_posts.text:
PPYMCA Summer Day Camp Week 2 — "Low-Key Famous Artist Week" — starts Monday, June 8 for ages 5–12.

Spots still available. Register at ppymca.org. Financial assistance is available.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: COS Childcare Desert — Eastern Colorado Springs Families Can't Find Care
stories.angle: El Paso County has 46,800 children under age 5 but child care facilities can serve only ~17,500 kids; eastern Colorado Springs — especially the southeast and Fountain-Fort Carson areas — is the hardest-hit "childcare desert"; average cost $16,300/year per child when care is found; parents are leaving the workforce because childcare costs exceed their earnings; child care advocacy groups launched a media campaign in January 2026; proposed solutions include more funding and reduced building renovation regulations

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
El Paso County: 46,800 children under age 5. Child care spots available: ~17,500.

Eastern Colorado Springs has the least access in the city — especially the southeast and Fountain-Fort Carson areas.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
The average annual cost of child care in Colorado Springs when you can find it: $16,300.

Many parents are doing the math and leaving the workforce.

The gap between supply and demand is 29,000+ kids.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Colorado Springs Childcare Desert: El Paso County Has 46,800 Kids Under 5 and Spots for 17,500

stories.title: D11 Labor Dispute — What Families Need to Know Heading into Summer and Fall
stories.angle: More than 300 days after the D11 Board of Education dissolved the 56-year-old master agreement with educators (December 2024 7-1 vote), the situation remains unresolved; May 2026 picket at D11 board meeting; October 2025 strike involved 1,000+ participants; families planning for the 2026-27 school year should understand the ongoing labor dispute; district has made some pay adjustments but no formal master agreement; families can follow updates at csea-cos.org

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
It's been 300+ days since D11 ended the 56-year master educator agreement.

A May 2026 picket showed this situation is far from resolved. What D11 families need to know for fall.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: D11 Labor Dispute: What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know Heading into the 2026–27 School Year
